up.method: UPLC (Waters AccQ amino acid analysis) of culture supernatant time-course (Fig 3)

sec.method: gas chromatography with flame photometric detector (GC-14B, ZO-1H column)

growth_records.cite: Hara, Takeshi et al. Interspecies metabolite transfer fuels the methionine metabolism of Fusobacterium nucleatum to stimulate volatile methyl mercaptan production. mSystems (2024). DOI:10.1128/msystems.00764-23. [PMC10878106]
growth_records.snippet: Fusobacterium nucleatum subsp. nucleatum ATCC25586 ... L-Methionine 477.71 ± 26.30 (ppm CH3SH). F. nucleatum gradually consumed methionine, about 50% in the substrate present during 12 h of incubation ... Glutamate present in the mCDM was f
growth_records.notes: Resting washed-cell suspension assay (OD600=1.0), NOT a growth culture; OD 'virtually unchanged' after 16 h (Table S2) so no growth_rate/doubling_time derivable (mu~0). CH3SH secretion 477.71+/-26.30 ppm is a headspace GC value at 5 mM L-methionine, pH 6.5, 16 h (Table 1); a comparable ~500 ppm plateau at 3.5-5.0 mM Met at pH 6.5 is shown in Fig 2A. Methionine uptake reported only as ~50% of the supplied substrate over 12 h (Fig 3); the exact starting methionine concentration for the Fig 3 time-course is not explicitly stated (mCDM was supplemented with 0.5-5.0 mM Met 'as necessary'), so an absolute mmol or mmol/gDW/h uptake rate cannot be computed without assumption; no gDW-per-OD conversion is given, precluding biomass-specific normalization. 13C5,15N-methionine tracing (10 mM, 6 h) was performed but reported as relative isotopomer abundances (m+n) over time, not net fluxes with units, so mfa_fluxes left empty.

growth_records.strain: F. nucleatum ATCC 25586 + S. gordonii DL1 WT

up.units: mM net over 12 h (inferred: 1.2 mM S. gordonii mono - 0.95 mM coculture)
up.method: UPLC; difference of ornithine released in S. gordonii monoculture vs coculture

sec.method: gas chromatography with flame photometric detector

growth_records.cite: Hara, Takeshi et al. Interspecies metabolite transfer fuels the methionine metabolism of Fusobacterium nucleatum to stimulate volatile methyl mercaptan production. mSystems (2024). DOI:10.1128/msystems.00764-23. [PMC10878106]
growth_records.snippet: cocultures of F. nucleatum and S. gordonii WT exhibited the highest level of consumption of methionine ... release of 1.2 and 0.95 mM ornithine, respectively ... suggesting an uptake of 0.25 mM ornithine by F. nucleatum ... approximately 2.
growth_records.notes: Coculture of two resting washed-cell suspensions (each OD600=1.0); no net growth reported (bacterial growth not significantly affected by ornithine addition, data not shown; OD unchanged). Net ornithine accumulation in coculture = 0.95 mM over 12 h -> 0.95/12 = 0.079 mM/h. Inferred F. nucleatum ornithine uptake = 1.2 mM (S. gordonii monoculture) - 0.95 mM (coculture) = 0.25 mM over 12 h, as stated verbatim by authors. CH3SH enhancement ~2.3-fold (0.5 mM Met) to ~3-fold (1 mM Met) vs monocultures; enhancement preserved in non-contact coculture (diffusible factor = ornithine). 13C5,15N-methionine intracellular tracing (CE-TOF-MS, 6 h) mapped fate of Met into methionine cycle and polyamine/salvage pathway but reported only relative isotopomer abundances (m+n), not net fluxes with units -> mfa_fluxes empty. Gene-expression: mgl/metK up 2.3-4.2-fold, metK/metH/metQ/metI up 4.0-5.6-fold in coculture (qPCR), consistent with enhanced Met uptake.
